title: polymeria lanata var. typica
intro: Polymeria lanata var. typica, also known as the woolly polymeria, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
